name: Cloudflare Workers API
what_it_does: Cloudflare Workers API for deploying, managing, and monitoring serverless edge compute functions running globally on Cloudflare's network with KV storage, Durable Objects, and R2.
use_cases: Deploying edge functions programmatically from CI/CD via agents
Managing Worker scripts, routes, and environment variables
Querying Workers Analytics and performance metrics
Managing KV namespaces and Durable Object state via API
Automating Cloudflare configuration changes across zones
not_for: Long-running compute jobs exceeding Workers CPU limits
Stateful applications requiring traditional databases (use Durable Objects or D1)
Teams needing Node.js-specific APIs not available in V8 isolates
best_when: An agent needs to deploy or manage Cloudflare Workers and related edge infrastructure as part of a DevOps or automation workflow.
avoid_when: You need long-running processes, GPU compute, or traditional server-based deployments.

auth.notes: API tokens with fine-grained permissions (recommended). Global API key available but not recommended. OAuth2 for third-party integrations. Token permissions are very granular.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 100,000 requests/day, 10ms CPU time per request
pricing.notes: Very generous free tier for Workers. Paid plan is $5/month for 10M requests — extremely cost-effective. KV, D1, and R2 have separate pricing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-structured error responses with error array containing code, message, and documentation URL. Error codes are unique and searchable. Excellent error documentation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Worker script uploads are idempotent by script name. KV operations support conditional updates with ETags. Configuration changes are idempotent by key.
known_agent_gotchas: Workers have a 10ms CPU time limit on free tier — agents must be aware of script complexity
V8 isolate environment lacks Node.js APIs — setTimeout, fetch, and Web APIs only
Account ID and Zone ID are both required depending on the operation type
Worker deployments via API require multipart/form-data with script content and metadata
KV consistency is eventually consistent — agents must handle stale reads
Durable Objects have different consistency guarantees than KV — understand the difference
